text_blocks.text: One of the most prominent Huguenot
refugees in the Netherlands was Pierre Bayle. He
started teaching in Rotterdam, where he finished
writing and publishing his multi-volume masterpiece,
Historical and Critical Dictionary. It became one
of the 100 foundational texts of the US Library of
Congress. Some Huguenot descendants in the
Netherlands may be noted by French family names,
although they typically use Dutch given names. Due
to the Huguenots' early ties with the leadership of
the Dutch Revolt and their own participation, some
of the Dutch patriciate are of part-Huguenot
descent. Some Huguenot families have kept alive
various traditions, such as the celebration and feast
of their patron Saint Nicolas, similar to the Dutch
